purpose: Test concept-only team-color and function markers on Quaternius intake GLBs before any runtime import decision.

concept_verdict.summary: Small external marker geometry appears sufficient to restore player ownership and role cues on Quaternius models without editing source GLBs. This is still a concept preview only; final runtime path requires footprint, selection ring, and fallback regression proof.

marker_concepts.concept: blue pole flag plus front banner
marker_concepts.purpose: Restore player ownership and main-base command cue at RTS camera distance.
marker_concepts.next_requirement: Place marker in a stable anchor or merged child mesh that does not shift selection ring or healthbar.

marker_concepts.concept: blue crest plus crossed weapon cue
marker_concepts.purpose: Recover S0 military/production readability while keeping Quaternius building form.
marker_concepts.next_requirement: Verify crest is visible from camera and does not imply unavailable upgrades.

marker_concepts.purpose: Give ownership without over-militarizing a civilian supply building.
marker_concepts.next_requirement: Keep marker small and confirm Farm remains visually subordinate to Town Hall/Barracks.

marker_concepts.concept: Mine body plus Resource Gold accent and gold light
marker_concepts.purpose: Combine cave/rock body with explicit gold resource cue.
marker_concepts.next_requirement: Turn into one selected/harvestable runtime object with worker approach lanes.

marker_concepts.concept: blue top flag plus attack-point cone
marker_concepts.purpose: Recover team ownership and defense role cue.
marker_concepts.next_requirement: Verify projectile spawn point and attack range visualization can align with marker.

next_gate: Build footprint/selection-ring/pathing proof using these marker placements.
Test GLB failure fallback while keeping marker paths deterministic.
Decide whether marker geometry should be separate runtime child objects or baked into a prepared GLB copy after approval.
